weather_line: Clear in the highlands · ducks incoming · brass instruments unsettled

stories.headline: Duck Bandit Identified: The Remaining Five Characters Resolve; a Fourth Duck Arrives at First Light; the Footprint Is a Friend's
body: At six forty-one this morning, Christine completed the decode. The Weasley Chromatic Cipher — discontinued 1987, still quietly alive among those who know — yielded the remaining five characters in eleven minutes of focused work and one cup of tea. The full seven-character sequence resolved, in order, to two initials and a trail name: the greeting of a fellow northeastern highland enthusiast, operating under a handle recognised immediately by Steve, who had last seen it carved into a waymarker post on the difficult left-hand section of the route they ran last Thursday.

The fourth duck arrived at five fifty-nine, before the household was properly awake — yellow, placed at the end of the established interval, completing the amber-green-red-yellow sequence that Steve confirmed is the traditional four-colour welcome used among a small and specific community of enchanted off-road travellers who've shared the northeastern hollows for years. The brass ducks on the shelf rotated to face the door and have not moved since.

The Ministry's recropped photograph — third crop, lower-left corner — shows a boot print consistent with a person who walks the way a person walks when they know exactly where they're going and are in no hurry at all. The Bandit is not unknown. The Bandit has been to the gorge. The Bandit, it turns out, left a duck on their rig three years ago on a different trail and never expected them to find it, and is apparently delighted to learn they did. Steve says this explains the first amber duck — the one that started all of this — completely. A note has been sent in return. The brass ducks are still facing the door.

stories.headline: Monday Returns to Gringotts; the Bank Receives It With Its Customary Lack of Ceremony; One Account Sorted Before the Corridor Clock Has Finished Its Opening Chime
body: The first business day following what the Duty Goblin has logged as 'the fortnight of accumulating incidents' opened without incident — a circumstance the Duty Goblin reportedly found more unsettling than the incidents themselves. Christine was at her desk at eight forty, had reviewed the pending correspondence by nine ten, and resolved a flagged discrepancy in the Harcourt Family Vault reclassification before the corridor clock completed its single Monday chime.

The Duty Goblin submitted a brief morning report describing conditions as 'stable, pending further review.' The further review is not scheduled. The third brass plaque remains where it was. The corridor is calm.

stories.headline: Warmest Lap Dispute Enters Third Week; a Ruling Is Issued; the Ruling Is Immediately Sat Upon
body: After twelve days of contested equilibrium, the Warmest Lap Commission — convened informally, quorate by accident — issued its ruling at ten past nine this morning: the lap in question is designated a shared resource, to be occupied on a rotating basis with no fixed schedule, adjudicated case by case. Jersey received this news from the warmest lap, where he had been for forty minutes. The ruling was placed on the side table beside him. He shifted seventeen degrees to the left and went back to sleep.
The Commission has noted the ruling's current location and described the situation as 'under ongoing review, structurally.'

stories.headline: Ministry Issues Monday Bulletin Confirming Monday; Notes That a New Week Has Begun; Describes This as 'an Opportunity'
body: The Ministry of Magic this morning confirmed that Monday, 20 July, is occurring, and that it represents the commencement of a new working week. The bulletin, three paragraphs in length, described the development as 'an opportunity for reset and forward momentum,' and attached a map that was, upon inspection, the previous map — unrotated, for the first time in several editions — with the words 'NEW WEEK' written across it in a confident hand.
The briefcase photograph was not included. Its absence was not explained. Several observers found the absence more notable than the photograph had been. No comment was offered.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Was present when the cipher was completed, having positioned herself two feet from the note at five forty-seven — eleven minutes before Christine sat down. She has since relocated to the kitchen and declined to be associated with any formal timeline.

Ellie: Studied the fourth duck for several minutes this morning with great attention and apparent recognition, then looked up to see if the duck had also noticed her. It had not. She found this acceptable.
Jersey: The Warmest Lap Commission ruling has been confirmed as comfortable. Jersey's posture on the matter has not changed and he does not expect it to.
founding_editor_note: The note came back, the duck arrived, and the thing resolved the way a well-built thing does — no drama, just square corners. I'll take partial credit. — D.

in_brief.sport: Monday morning training declared 'a return to form' by the one person present, who is also the judge.
in_brief.forecast: Clear skies through midweek · a fifth colour is not anticipated · the brass ducks will remain where they are, and that is, on reflection, exactly right.

storyline_updates.title: The Duck Bandit Stirs Again
storyline_updates.summary: Resolved Monday morning: the seven-character cipher decoded fully via the Weasley Chromatic Cipher to initials and a trail name — a fellow northeastern highland enthusiast known to Steve from a waymarker post on the difficult left-hand section. The fourth duck (yellow) arrived at five fifty-nine, completing the amber-green-red-yellow traditional four-colour greeting sequence. The Ministry's footprint matched a person who knew exactly where they were going. The Bandit is not a stranger; they left a duck three years ago and are delighted it was eventually found. A return note has been sent. The brass ducks face the door. Arc closed with full honours.

storyline_updates.title: The Gringotts Efficiency Inquiry
storyline_updates.summary: New arc opening: the Gringotts Senior Audit Subcommittee has quietly initiated a review of Liaison Wing efficiency following the fortnight of accumulating incidents. No one has told Christine. The Duty Goblin has been asked to maintain a log. He has begun the log. The log's first entry reads: 'Monday. Corridor clock chimed once. Harcourt matter resolved before chime completed. Duration of chime: four seconds.' Day 2 should establish that Christine becomes aware of the audit, probably through means the Subcommittee did not intend.
